qa_pairs.question: When will invitation emails for the Fast Track process be sent to eligible Principal Investigators (PIs)?
qa_pairs.answer: Invitation emails will be sent to eligible PIs in mid-September 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What is the opening date for Fast Track submissions on CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCDB will open for Fast Track submissions on September 23, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for submitting Fast Track applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The deadline to submit Fast Track applications is November 4, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to extend the Fast Track application deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: No, extending this deadline is not possible.

qa_pairs.question: When can PIs expect to receive notification with the results of the Fast Track competition?
qa_pairs.answer: Notification with the results of the competition will be sent to PIs in late March 2026.

qa_pairs.question: When will new resource allocations be implemented?
qa_pairs.answer: New allocations will be implemented in early April 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to apply through the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: PIs with an existing RRG award who meet the Fast Track eligibility are allowed to submit a lightweight progress report to renew their request for computational resources.

qa_pairs.question: Does applying through the Fast Track process guarantee the same allocation as the previous year?
qa_pairs.answer: No, applying through the Fast Track process does not guarantee that you will receive the same allocation as last year.

qa_pairs.question: How is the resource request scaled for Fast Track applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The score received in your original RRG application will be reused, and your resource request will be scaled using the same function used for all other applications according to demand and supply.

qa_pairs.question: Can the resulting allocation from a Fast Track application be different from the previous year?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the resulting allocation may be scaled by a different factor than last year, which could lead to either an increase or a decrease in allocated resources for your project compared to last year.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum science score required for a previous RRG application to be eligible for the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: The previous RRG application must have received a science score greater than 2.0 points (out of 5).

qa_pairs.question: For how many consecutive years can an application use the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: This process can be used for no more than two consecutive years for a given application.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I meet the Fast Track eligibility criteria but did not receive an invitation?
qa_pairs.answer: If you believe you meet the eligibility criteria, yet did not receive an invitation to Fast Track, you should email allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What are the benefits of the Fast Track Process?
qa_pairs.answer: The benefits of the Fast Track Process include a lightweight application, no additional peer-review, and the ability to apply for up to 2 consecutive years.

qa_pairs.question: Do Fast Track applications undergo a scientific review?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Fast Track applications receive a technical review but do not receive another scientific review.

qa_pairs.question: How do eligible PIs receive an invitation to Fast Track?
qa_pairs.answer: Eligible PIs will receive an email with an invitation to Fast Track in September.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the Fast Track application form?
qa_pairs.answer: The Fast Track application form will show by default on CCDB at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/resource_allocation_competitions/applications.

qa_pairs.question: How do I start working on a Fast Track application and submit it?
qa_pairs.answer: To start working on it, click on 'Edit' and then 'Submit' when you are ready.

qa_pairs.question: What if I want to decline the Fast Track invitation and submit a full RRG application instead?
qa_pairs.answer: To decline the invitation to Fast Track and submit a full RRG application instead, click on 'Change to RRG'.

qa_pairs.question: Can I switch back to a Fast Track application if I initially chose to submit a full RRG application?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you change your mind later on, you can click on 'Change to Fast Track' to continue working on your Fast Track application.

qa_pairs.question: Can I delegate access to another user for my Fast Track application?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can click on 'Delegate' to grant access to another user with an active account with the Alliance.

qa_pairs.question: Who is responsible for the application and resource utilization when an application is delegated?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI remains responsible for the application submitted and the utilization of the computational resources allocated, even if a delegated user submits it.

qa_pairs.question: What information is required in the 'Details' section of the Fast Track 2026 application form?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Details' section requires justification for any change requested compared to the eligible amount of resources communicated in the invitation email, and a list and description of any issues with the allocation or 'No issue to report'.

qa_pairs.question: Are changes requested in the Fast Track application guaranteed to be fulfilled?
qa_pairs.answer: No, while efforts will be made to make reasonable accommodations, fulfilment of change requests is not guaranteed.

qa_pairs.question: What modifications to current allocations are allowed in the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: Allowed modifications include a change of resource location, a 10% increase in cloud compute and storage resources, and a 10% increase of PROJECT, NEARLINE, and/or dCache storage resources up to a maximum of 50 TB.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to migrate current allocation from one system to another?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a change of resource location (i.e., request to migrate current allocation from one system to another) is allowed.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum percentage increase allowed for cloud compute and storage resources?
qa_pairs.answer: A 10% increase in cloud compute and storage resources is allowed.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum allowed increase for PROJECT, NEARLINE, and/or dCache storage resources?
qa_pairs.answer: A 10% increase of PROJECT, NEARLINE and/or dCache storage resources is allowed, up to a maximum of 50 TB of additional storage based on the eligible amount communicated in the invitation email.

qa_pairs.question: Can I request an increase in CPU or GPU resources through the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: No, an increase of CPU or GPU requests is not allowed.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I need more CPU or GPU resources?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need more compute resources (CPU or GPU), you must submit a new application.

qa_pairs.question: If I was allowed 100 TB of project storage in the invitation email, what is the maximum new storage request I can make?
qa_pairs.answer: You may request 10 TB more, making your new storage request 110 TB.

qa_pairs.question: If my invitation email allowed 1,000 TB of nearline storage, what is the maximum additional storage I can request through Fast Track?
qa_pairs.answer: The maximum you can request is 50 TB more, even though 10% would be 100 TB, because the request increase is capped at 50 TB. Your new storage request would then be 1,050 TB.

qa_pairs.question: What information should be included in the 'Progress report' section of the Fast Track application form?
qa_pairs.answer: Details about what has been accomplished with the resources used (e.g., publications) should be provided in the 'Progress report' section.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I email if I have questions about the Fast Track process?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have questions about the Fast Track process, please email allocations@tech.alliancecan.ca.
